html:
Exclusive to:

The Tar Suit is a craftable Dress Item exclusive to the Shipwrecked DLC. It is found in the Dress Tab, requires a Science Machine to prototype, and 4 Tar, 2 Palm Leaves, and 2 Cloth to craft. The Sewing Kit doesn't repair its durability, instead it can be repaired with tar restoring 25% durability.

When worn during Rain, the player's Wetness will not increase at all.

Trivia

  • The Tar Suit was added to the Shipwrecked DLC more than a year after its official completion in the Home Sea Home update, after being tested in a beta branch for several months.
  • The Tar Suit is most likely made out of tar paper, which was commonly used in Hoovervilles to keep out rain from cardboard houses.

Gallery

  • Wilson wearing a Tar Suit.
  • Willow wearing a Tar Suit.
  • Wolfgang wearing a Tar Suit.
  • Wendy wearing a Tar Suit.
  • WX-78 wearing a Tar Suit.
  • Wickerbottom wearing a Tar Suit.
  • Woodie wearing a Tar Suit.
  • Wes wearing a Tar Suit.
  • Maxwell wearing a Tar Suit.
  • Wigfrid wearing a Tar Suit.
  • Webber wearing a Tar Suit.
  • Walani wearing a Tar Suit.
  • Warly wearing a Tar Suit.
  • Wilbur wearing a Tar Suit.
  • Woodlegs wearing a Tar Suit.
  • Wilba wearing a Tar Suit.
  • Wormwood wearing a Tar Suit.
  • Wheeler wearing a Tar Suit.
  • Tar Suit as seen in the poster for the Home Sea Home update.

"tarsuit"
“I'll pitch a fit if I have to wear that.”
–Wilson
“This might not be the safest thing to light fires in.”
–Willow
“Water not get Wolfgang now.”
–Wolfgang
“To make my outside match my inside.”
–Wendy
“KEEP THE WATER OUT. KEEP IT OUTTT”
–WX-78
“Effective, but not very durable.”
–Wickerbottom
“You do what you gotta to survive.”
–Woodie
“I hope this won't tar-nish my dapper image.”
–Maxwell
“An entirely effective rain suit! Could be a bit more durable.”
–Wagstaff
“This shall repel Aegir's clammy töuch.”
–Wigfrid
“Oh, oh yuck! We'll never get that out of our fur!”
–Webber
“This may have been my worst idea. Ever.”
–Walani
“I hope there are no feathers around!”
–Warly
“Y'arr, cov'r me in tarr!”
–Woodlegs
“MOST EXCELLENT FANCY SUIT”
–Wilba
“Feelin' gloopy”
–Wormwood
“I like it. It says I'm willing to wear anything.”
–Wheeler
The Tar Suit is a craftable Dress Item exclusive to the Shipwrecked DLC. It is found in the Dress Tab, requires a Science Machine to prototype, and 4 Tar, 2 Palm Leaves, and 2 Cloth to craft. The Sewing Kit doesn't repair its durability, instead it can be repaired with tar restoring 25% durability.
When worn during Rain, the player's Wetness will not increase at all.

The Tar Suit was added to the Shipwrecked DLC more than a year after its official completion in the Home Sea Home update, after being tested in a beta branch for several months.
The Tar Suit is most likely made out of tar paper, which was commonly used in Hoovervilles to keep out rain from cardboard houses.

The '''Tar Suit''' is a craftable [[Dress]] [[Item]] exclusive to the ''[[Don't Starve: Shipwrecked|Shipwrecked]]'' DLC. It is found in the [[Dress|Dress Tab]], requires a [[Science Machine]] to prototype, and 4 [[Tar]], 2 [[Palm Leaf|Palm Leaves]], and 2 [[Cloth]] to craft. The [[Sewing Kit]] doesn't repair its durability, instead it can be repaired with [[tar]] restoring 25% durability.

When worn during [[Rain]], the player's [[Wetness]] will not increase at all.

=={{Pic32|Placeholder}} Trivia==
* The Tar Suit was added to the ''Shipwrecked'' DLC more than a year after its official completion in the ''[[Don't Starve: Shipwrecked#November 21, 2017 - Home Sea Home Update|Home Sea Home]]'' update, after being tested in a beta branch for several months.
*The Tar Suit is most likely made out of [[wikipedia:Tar_paper|tar paper]], which was commonly used in Hoovervilles to keep out rain from cardboard houses.
